## Summary

- what changed
- why it changed

## Contract Surface

- schema, ABI, action catalog, conformance, registry, docs, or package surface touched
- RFC or issue linked when the public contract changes

## Compatibility

- additive, breaking, clarification-only, or implementation-only
- migration note added when needed

## Validation

- commands run
- exact outcome
- if something was not run, why

## Generated Artifacts

- schemas regenerated through `backend/scripts/generate_spec_schemas.py` when model contracts changed
- TypeScript `dist` rebuilt when package source changed
- generated files left untouched when not relevant

## Trust And Boundary Checks

- [ ] no private Warlog runtime behavior was documented as part of the public spec
- [ ] conformance or registry claims are backed by reproducible evidence
- [ ] AI, approval, provenance, dry-run/live-run, and audit-chain trust boundaries were preserved
- [ ] live-validation claims include real tenant or lab evidence, or are not made
- [ ] docs avoid brittle branch-specific test counts unless generated by CI
